title: Intense Smoky American Peppercorn Flatbread
description: A smoky, shaggy flatbread with peppercorn crumble, baked till puffed, FODMAP-friendly and gluten-free.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together flour, salt, baking powder, and smoked paprika.
Make a well in the center and add water, oil, and maple syrup; mix until a soft dough forms.
Turn dough onto a lightly oiled surface and knead for 2 minutes until smooth.
Divide dough into 4 equal portions, roll each into a thin circle, and let rest for 5 minutes to relax the gluten.
For each circle, sprinkle a thin layer of cheddar and peppercorns, then fold the edges to create a shaggy fold and seal.
Place the folded dough on a parchment-lined baking sheet, brush the tops with a little oil, and dot with extra peppercorns.
Bake uncovered at 220°C for 13 minutes until puffed and golden.
Remove from oven, cool slightly, and slice into wedges.
Serve warm or at room temperature with a side of low FODMAP salsa.
